what_it_does: Defines a systemd timer that activates company-claude-sessions-reindex.service at 5, 25, and 45 minutes past every hour, with Persistent=true, and installs into company.target.
observations: OnCalendar is set to *:05/20, producing three evenly spaced triggers per hour offset by 5 minutes
Persistent=true means a missed trigger will run when the system comes back up
Unit= points to a separate service that performs the actual reindex work
WantedBy=company.target ties the timer to a custom target
Description explicitly states the 5-minute offset is intentional relative to an exporter running at *:00/20
standouts: The deliberate 5-minute offset after the exporter cadence to avoid racing the export
Use of Persistent=true for catch-up execution
notes: A 20-minute systemd timer offset by 5 minutes so reindexing runs after each Claude-session export window.
questions: What exactly does company-claude-sessions-reindex.service do when triggered?
What is company.target and which other units belong to it?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the OnCalendar schedule: it fires ~5 minutes after each *:00/20 export window.
intention_signals: It is trying to schedule reindexing shortly after each export window so the reindexer sees the transcripts produced in that window without colliding with the exporter.
novelty: The explicit 5-minute offset relative to a sibling exporter cadence is a deliberate pipeline timing detail rather than a plain periodic schedule.
contribution: Provides a persistent, offset trigger schedule that reliably starts reindexing after each export window.
summary_for_embedding: A systemd timer unit that fires company-claude-sessions-reindex.service at 5, 25, and 45 minutes past every hour with Persistent=true.
intention_for_embedding: To reindex Claude sessions roughly five minutes after each 20-minute export window so the latest transcripts are included.
